title: Is a Node.js ≥ 20 prerequisite acceptable for the target non-technical user?
description: ADR-P6-001/ADR-P6-006 make the Work HQ opt-in via npm scripts, and ADR-P6-004 makes the wizard the recommended onboarding path. For a Head-of-Product-Operations audience, 'install Node, run npm install' may be the single highest-friction step in the whole system — potentially higher than the 30-minute M5 onboarding budget allows. Decide: (a) accept the prerequisite and document a 5-minute Node setup preflight in README; (b) keep chat-only onboarding (os-helper 'onboard') as the documented PRIMARY path with the wizard positioned as an enhancement for those who have Node; (c) invest in a zero-install packaging later (e.g. a single-binary build) if adoption data shows Node is the drop-off point. This changes README framing and the M6 acceptance criteria, not the architecture.
resolution: Accept the prerequisite, but delegate it to the AI: the README is designed to be AI-assistant-friendly. The non-technical user points their AI assistant at the README and the assistant runs all the prerequisites (Node.js install, npm install, first launch) on their behalf — explicit, ordered, copy-runnable steps. Chat-only onboarding remains the documented no-Node fallback. ADR-P6-001, ADR-P6-004, and ADR-P6-006 updated.
